title: 3. ETL pipeline — extract, build ABox, validate, promote
description: How knowledge gets into Neo4j safely. Memorize the chant and why each gate exists.
story:
Knowledge does not appear magically in chat. It is loaded through a pipeline:

1. EXTRACT sources (PIM catalog, FSM work orders, claims, CRM assets, files).
2. TRANSFORM into one catalog shaped like our ABox (OntologyBuilder).
3. VALIDATE against TBox shapes (min symptoms, links must point to real ids).
4. LOAD into staging Neo4j (:7688) with MERGE.
5. SMOKE test a few diagnosis phrases.
6. PROMOTE to production Neo4j (:7687) — only then does chat see it.
7. INVALIDATE caches so old subgraphs die.

Operator chant (memorize order):
Sources → Fetch → Select → Validate ABox → Materialize → Smoke → Approve → Promote staging → Promote production.
one_liner: Extract → ABox build → validate → staging → smoke → production → drop caches.
why_it_matters: Chat must never read half-baked knowledge (staging exists for that).
Selection-scoped runs let you onboard one SKU without rewriting the fleet.
say_aloud: Extract pulls from source systems; transform maps fields into shared TBox classes as ABox.
We validate shapes before materialize and promote.
Staging is bolt 7688; production is bolt 7687; chat reads production only.
After load we invalidate named caches so readers see fresh graph data.
A pipeline run can be dry-run to preview without writing Neo4j.

beats.title: Memorize the operator sequence
beats.narrative: If you can chant this, you understand the control plane.
beats.code:
Sources
  → Fetch          (preview delta / dry extract)
  → Select         (which product_ids this batch)
  → Validate ABox  (shapes vs TBox)
  → Materialize    (upsert catalog for selection)
  → Smoke          (run known diagnosis phrases)
  → Approve
  → Promote staging   (:7688)
  → Promote production (:7687)
  → Invalidate caches
beats.say_after: Chat never reads staging. Promote is the door.

annotations.note: Fail here = no bad data later.
Stale cache is a silent bug.

line_quiz.why: Diagnose path is production-only by design.

pro_tips: TRICK: keep stages idempotent & replayable — a failed promote can be re-run without double-writing (Kleppmann, DDIA).
GOTCHA: chat must read only the PROMOTED graph, never staging — promotion is the single gate.
TRICK: tag facts with a named graph / source so you can promote or roll back a whole source atomically.

beats.title: Pipeline registry ids (as-built names)
beats.goal: Know the real pipeline names in this repo.
beats.narrative: These ids show up in Admin / control plane.
beats.code:
structured_extract      # PIM / FSM / Claims / CRM connectors
semi_structured_ingest   # CSV / JSONL
unstructured_extract     # manuals / notes patterns
preprocess_normalize     # clean / provisional notes
knowledge_materialize    # OntologyBuilder → catalog (selection)
smoke_validate           # diagnosis scenarios
promote_graph            # MERGE Neo4j staging | production
bootstrap_all            # chain for first build
incremental_sync         # chain for ongoing updates
beats.say_after: Materialize builds ABox; promote_graph writes Neo4j.

annotations.note: This is where ABox is assembled.
This is the graph load step.

line_quiz.prompt: Which step builds the ABox catalog?

fill_blanks.template:
{{a}} builds catalog ABox
{{b}} MERGEs into Neo4j

beats.title: Code spine (tiny mental model)
beats.goal: Map words to functions — not every file.
beats.narrative: Extract can be parallel (I/O). Transform stays serial (deterministic merges).
beats.code:
# conceptual spine (names match the platform idea)
records = extract_sources(product_ids)          # I/O — may use thread pool
catalog = ontology_builder.build(records)       # serial transform → ABox
errors  = validate_shapes(catalog)              # fail-closed
if errors:
    raise ValueError(errors)
if not dry_run:
    populate_graph(catalog, env="staging")      # MERGE :7688
    run_smoke(product_ids)
    populate_graph(catalog, env="production")   # MERGE :7687
    invalidate_all_named_caches()
beats.say_after: Parallel extract is OK. Parallel transform is usually not.

annotations.note: Connectors wait on network — threads help.
Caches must die after graph changes.

line_quiz.why: Waiting on HTTP/files benefits from concurrency.

line_quiz.answer: Readers must not see stale subgraphs
choices: Readers must not see stale subgraphs

fill_blanks.template:
catalog = ontology_builder.{{fn}}(records)
if not dry_run:
    populate_graph(catalog, env="{{env}}")
    {{inv}}()

concepts.definition: Staging for safe MERGE; production for diagnose reads.

concepts.say_aloud: Shapes run before materialize and promote.

concepts.definition: Run pipeline for chosen product_ids only.
concepts.say_aloud: One SKU batch without rewriting the fleet.

common_mistakes: Calling 'Fetch' an ontology build — Fetch previews sources/delta.
final_boss: Recite the operator chant start to finish.
Draw Extract → Transform → Validate → Stage → Prod on paper.
Name three pipeline registry ids and what they do.
